comment_pin: allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly + allowManagedHooksOnly MUST sit at this TOP level: claude-code reads them ONLY at the settings root (verified against the shipped binary v2.1.205 at every read site); nested inside permissions{} they are silently INERT — which is how a stray user-layer ask rule stalled an unattended run on 2026-07-09. With the pin LIVE, user/project/local/cliArg/session permission rules (allow+ask+deny) AND user-layer hooks are ALL ignored — so everything the loop needs lives in THIS file: the gate hook, the stall-visibility loggers, the one destructive ask, and the tool allows. Session-added ask rules (the stall class) are structurally impossible.

permissions.comment_allow: Full-autonomy (make-it-work stage): allow ALL Bash + the file/orchestration tools so the autonomous loop never prompts (with rules pinned managed-only, no user-layer allow list backstops these). The deny[] below still hard-blocks its entries (deny > allow), and the gate-push.sh PreToolUse hook still runs (hooks fire regardless of allow rules) so every push-to-main / merge verb still routes to Arthur's click. The sole surviving ask beyond the gate is gh repo delete — never on a loop path; there a stall beats execution. Tighten back to a scoped list in the make-it-safe stage.
